onboarding_success_created_key_text.description: Instructions for users who created a new key
onboarding_success_created_key_text.message:
Check your inbox for an email from the Mailvelope key server.
Decrypt it using the password of your newly created key.
Open the verification link inside. This makes your public key available to others, so they can send you encrypted messages on Mailvelope.

onboarding_success_imported_key_text.description: Instructions for users who imported an existing key
onboarding_success_imported_key_text.message:
Open your email provider and write an encrypted message.
To let others find your key automatically, you can upload it to the Mailvelope key server.

provider_gmail_dialog_auth_intro.message: In order to use the Gmail integration for <0></0>, the following permissions must be granted to Mailvelope:

provider_gmail_dialog_auth_outro.message: If you click on the sign in button, a Google authorization window opens. Select your Gmail account for the email address <0></0> and follow the instructions.

provider_gmail_dialog_description.description: Leading text for GMAIL API dialog.
provider_gmail_dialog_description.message: Using the Gmail API extends the functionality of Mailvelope with Gmail and simplifies sending and reading encrypted emails and attachments.

provider_gmail_dialog_gsuite_alert.description: Alert text for GSuite Users in GMAIL API dialog.
provider_gmail_dialog_gsuite_alert.message: This feature is free for Gmail accounts. If you are part of a Google Workspace (<0>workspace.google.com</0>) organization, you will need to purchase a license from the <1>Mailvelope website</1>.

provider_gmail_integration_info.description: Info message for Gmail integration option
provider_gmail_integration_info.message: Mailvelope requires additional permissions to access the Gmail API. As soon as you use the encryption functions of Mailvelope in Gmail, you are guided through an authorization process. This requires you to sign in to your Google account.

provider_gmail_integration_warning.description: Warning message, when Gmail is not authorized
provider_gmail_integration_warning.message: Gmail is not authorized with the search pattern <0></0>. Please check the settings in <1></1>.

provider_gmail_licensing_dialog_business.description: Info text for price label of Business button

provider_gmail_licensing_dialog_deactiva.description: Gmail API deactivate button in licensing dialog

provider_gmail_licensing_dialog_para_1.description: Text of Gmail API licensing dialog first paragraph.
provider_gmail_licensing_dialog_para_1.message: Use of the Gmail API within Google Workspace organizations is <0>subject to a fee</0>.

provider_gmail_licensing_dialog_para_2.description: Text of Gmail API licensing dialog second paragraph.
provider_gmail_licensing_dialog_para_2.message: You can continue to use Mailvelope with Gmail for free, but <0>with the API your workflow</0> for writing and reading encrypted emails and their attachments will be <1>much faster and easier</1>.

provider_gmail_licensing_dialog_para_3.description: Text of Gmail API licensing dialog third paragraph.
provider_gmail_licensing_dialog_para_3.message: Test Mailvelope Business with activated Gmail API <0>14 days for free</0>:

provider_gmail_licensing_dialog_test_btn.description: Gmail API test button in licensing dialog

provider_gmail_licensing_dialog_title.description: Title of GMAIL API licensing dialog.

provider_gmail_licensing_table_caption.description: Caption for G Suite licensing table.
provider_gmail_licensing_table_caption.message: Licenses to use the Gmail API integration within Google Workspace (<0>workspace.google.com</0>) can be purchased from the <1>Mailvelope website</1>.

provider_gmail_licensing_table_title.description: Title for G Suite licensing table.

provider_gmail_mismatch_cancel_btn.description: Cancel button in the Gmail account mismatch dialog.

provider_gmail_mismatch_intro.description: Intro line in the Gmail account mismatch dialog. Sits above the comparison block which already shows both addresses.
provider_gmail_mismatch_intro.message: Mailvelope expects a different Gmail account than the one Google authorized.

provider_gmail_mismatch_label_actual.description: Label for the actually-authorized (wrong) account in the Gmail account mismatch comparison.

provider_gmail_mismatch_label_expected.description: Label for the expected (correct) account in the Gmail account mismatch comparison.

provider_gmail_mismatch_retry_btn.description: Retry button in the Gmail account mismatch dialog.

provider_gmail_mismatch_step1.description: First recovery step in the Gmail account mismatch dialog.
provider_gmail_mismatch_step1.message: Click "Try again" below to reopen Google's account chooser.

provider_gmail_mismatch_step2.description: Second recovery step. Placeholder is the expected email.
provider_gmail_mismatch_step2.message: Pick <0></0>. If it isn't listed, choose "Use another account" and sign in.

provider_gmail_mismatch_title.description: Title of the Gmail account mismatch dialog.

provider_gmail_mismatch_why_body.description: Cause explanation revealed when the user expands the disclosure in the Gmail account mismatch dialog.
provider_gmail_mismatch_why_body.message: Chrome may be signed in with a different default Google account, or a different account was chosen on Google's sign-in screen.

provider_gmail_mismatch_why_summary.description: Disclosure summary above the cause explanation in the Gmail account mismatch dialog.

pwd_dialog_cache_pwd.description: Checkbox label for remembering of the password.

pwd_dialog_reason_add_user.description: Reason shown in the password dialog when adding a user ID to a key.
pwd_dialog_reason_add_user.message: Please enter your key password to create the user ID.

pwd_dialog_reason_create_backup.description: Reason shown in the password dialog when creating a recovery sheet.
pwd_dialog_reason_create_backup.message: Please enter your key password to create a recovery sheet.

pwd_dialog_reason_create_draft.description: Reason shown in the password dialog when saving a draft.
pwd_dialog_reason_create_draft.message: Enter key password to save the draft.

pwd_dialog_reason_decrypt.description: Reason shown in the password dialog when decrypting a message.
pwd_dialog_reason_decrypt.message: Please enter your key password to decrypt this message.

pwd_dialog_reason_editor.description: Reason shown in the password dialog when updating encrypted communication settings.
pwd_dialog_reason_editor.message: Please enter your key password to update settings for encrypted communication.

pwd_dialog_reason_revoke.description: Reason shown in the password dialog when revoking a key.
pwd_dialog_reason_revoke.message: Please enter your key password to revoke this key.

pwd_dialog_reason_revoke_user.description: Reason shown in the password dialog when revoking a user ID.
pwd_dialog_reason_revoke_user.message: Please enter your key password to revoke the user ID.

pwd_dialog_reason_set_exdate.description: Reason shown in the password dialog when setting a new key expiration date.
pwd_dialog_reason_set_exdate.message: Please enter your key password to set a new expiration date for the key.

pwd_dialog_reason_sign.description: Reason shown in the password dialog when signing a message.
pwd_dialog_reason_sign.message: Please enter your key password to sign this message.

recovery_sheet_backup_data.description: Subheading on the recovery sheet introducing what is backed up.

recovery_sheet_backup_local.description: Recovery sheet note about storing the backup locally; $1 is the provider name.
recovery_sheet_backup_local.message: If you would like to exclusively save your data locally on your computer, you will find this option in the encrypted communication settings of your $1 account.

recovery_sheet_backup_server.description: Recovery sheet note about storing the encrypted backup on provider servers; $1 is the provider name.
recovery_sheet_backup_server.message: To back up the data that is important for encrypted communication (e.g. key password), the data will be saved encrypted on our $1 servers by our security partner Mailvelope.

recovery_sheet_check_settings.description: Instruction on the recovery sheet pointing to the account settings path.
recovery_sheet_check_settings.message: Please check the settings in your account under:

recovery_sheet_creation_date.description: Label for recovery sheet creation date

recovery_sheet_data_lost.description: Warning on the recovery sheet about losing access to messages without a backup.
recovery_sheet_data_lost.message: Without a backup, your encrypted messages cannot be decrypted again in case you lose your key password or in case of a defective device or problems with the browser extension!

recovery_sheet_enter_code.message: For the installation on other computers, enter the recovery code in the settings under:

recovery_sheet_explain_pgp.description: Explanation of PGP on the recovery sheet; $1 is the provider name.
recovery_sheet_explain_pgp.message: Encrypted communication with $1 is a PGP-based procedure. It enables you to send encrypted emails and, at the same time, ensures that only you and the recipient are able to read them.

recovery_sheet_extension_problems.message: because of problems with the browser extension

recovery_sheet_forgot_password.message: because you forgot the password to your key

recovery_sheet_further_info.description: Pointer to more information on the recovery sheet; $1 is the provider name.
recovery_sheet_further_info.message: You can find more information and setting options in your $1 account under

recovery_sheet_invite_contacts.description: Recovery sheet note on inviting contacts to encrypted communication; $1 is the provider name.
recovery_sheet_invite_contacts.message: In your encrypted communication settings of your $1 account, you have the possibility to invite additional contacts to use encrypted communication via PGP. Contacts that have already been invited successfully can be recognized by the padlock icon next to the email address.

recovery_sheet_keep_safe.description: Recovery sheet recommendation to keep the printed sheet in a safe place.
recovery_sheet_keep_safe.message: Store a print-out of this document in a safe place and make sure no one else has access to the recovery code.

recovery_sheet_key_server.description: Recovery sheet note about the provider key directory; $1 is the provider name.
recovery_sheet_key_server.message: Other $1 users who are using the PGP procedure of GMX can be directly found in a central directory in your $1 account. If you do not want to have any entries in this directory, please deactivate this function in your $1 account settings.

recovery_sheet_mobile_devices.description: Heading on the recovery sheet for the mobile devices section.

recovery_sheet_not_working.message: If your encrypted communication is no longer working, e.g.:

recovery_sheet_other_computer.description: Heading on the recovery sheet for the other-computer section.

recovery_sheet_other_contacts.description: Section heading on the recovery sheet about communicating with other contacts.

recovery_sheet_other_devices_setup.description: Section heading on the recovery sheet about setting up other devices.
recovery_sheet_other_devices_setup.message: Setting up encrypted communication on other devices

recovery_sheet_pgp_compat.description: Recovery sheet note that secure communication requires PGP on both sides.
recovery_sheet_pgp_compat.message: You can communicate securely with other contacts if they are also using encrypted communication (PGP).

recovery_sheet_print_block.message: Please store this document in a safe place!

recovery_sheet_print_button.description: Button on the recovery sheet that opens the browser print dialog.

recovery_sheet_print_notice.message: Please print this document and store it in a safe place!

recovery_sheet_provider_communication.description: Recovery sheet label for the provider settings path (communication entry).

recovery_sheet_provider_inbox.description: Recovery sheet label for the provider settings path (inbox entry).

recovery_sheet_provider_security.description: Recovery sheet label for the provider settings path (security entry).

recovery_sheet_provider_settings.description: Recovery sheet label for the provider settings path (settings entry).

recovery_sheet_qr_code.description: Instruction on the recovery sheet next to the QR code.
recovery_sheet_qr_code.message: Scan the QR code with your mobile device.

recovery_sheet_recommendation.description: Inline label introducing a recommendation on the recovery sheet.

recovery_sheet_recovery_code.description: Heading on the recovery sheet above the recovery code.

recovery_sheet_trusted_contacts.description: Recovery sheet note about verifying the identity of contacts.
recovery_sheet_trusted_contacts.message: With GMX contacts you can be sure that the users displayed are in fact valid users. You should, however, always verify the identity of contacts with an email address from another email provider.

recovery_sheet_unknown_third.description: Recovery sheet warning to only set up encrypted communication on trusted devices.
recovery_sheet_unknown_third.message: Only set up encrypted communication on secure devices that cannot be accessed by strangers.

recovery_sheet_unlock_backup.description: Recovery sheet note that the backup can only be decrypted with the recovery code.
recovery_sheet_unlock_backup.message: The backup of your data can only be decrypted with the help of the recovery code on this recovery sheet.

reload_tab.message: Please reload the corresponding pages for the settings to take effect.

restore_password_dialog_headline.description: Heading shown after a successful restore, above the recovered key password.

security_background_icons_text.message: 1. Choose an icon that you like

security_background_text.message: Your security background is displayed behind every encrypted email. To make it really safe, you should personalize it. This is how you ensure, that no one is able to reconstruct the Mailvelope interface to access your personal data.
